skill.name: azure-monitor-ingestion-java
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Java to send custom logs through Data Collection Rules and Data Collection Endpoints.
skill.summary: Build Java log ingestion flows for Azure Monitor custom logs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but SKILL.md contains documentation snippets and Java examples rather than executable skill code. The hardcoded URLs, relative link, weak-crypto alerts, Azure credential alert, and heuristic combinations are false positives in context; the only confirmed risk is intentional Azure Monitor network ingestion guidance.

low_findings.title: Intentional Azure Monitor Network Ingestion
low_findings.description: The skill shows Java code that uploads logs to a configured Azure Monitor Data Collection Endpoint. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid sending secrets or sensitive log fields.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Java examples directly call upload methods against a configured Azure ingestion endpoint. The context is legitimate Azure Monitor SDK guidance, so this is a low operational data-handling risk rather than malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Send Java Logs to Azure Monitor
content.value_statement: Java teams need reliable examples for sending custom logs to Azure Monitor. This skill provides focused SDK guidance for clients, DCR identifiers, upload options, async ingestion, and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for azure-monitor-ingestion.
Explains required Azure Monitor resources for log ingestion.
Demonstrates synchronous and asynchronous LogsIngestionClient creation.
Provides upload examples for custom logs, concurrency, and partial failures.
Includes a simple Java log entry model for JSON serialization.
Links to Azure SDK, product documentation, and troubleshooting resources.
limitations: Does not create Azure resources or Data Collection Rules.
Does not validate workspace schemas or stream names automatically.
Does not include production retry, masking, or batching policies.
Requires Azure credentials and permissions configured outside the skill.

use_cases.title: Add Custom Application Logs
use_cases.description: Use the Java SDK examples to send structured application events into Azure Monitor custom tables.

use_cases.title: Tune High-Volume Ingestion
use_cases.description: Apply concurrency and async upload guidance when a service needs higher log throughput.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Ingestion Failures
use_cases.description: Use the error handling patterns to inspect HTTP responses and diagnose DCR or endpoint issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a Java Azure Monitor LogsIngestionClient using DefaultAzureCredential and a Data Collection Endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Upload Custom Logs
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me adapt the Azure Monitor Ingestion Java example to upload custom log objects to my DCR stream.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Partial Failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Java error handling for Azure Monitor log ingestion that records failed entries and keeps valid batches moving.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Async High Throughput
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Java ingestion design and recommend async client, concurrency, batching, and retry choices for high-volume Azure Monitor logs.

output_examples.input: I need to send Java application events to a custom Azure Monitor table.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering prerequisites, client creation, DCR identifiers, stream naming, and a first upload flow.

output_examples.input: My upload returns authorization and not found errors.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist that separates permission problems from endpoint, rule, and stream configuration issues.

output_examples.input: We expect large log batches from a reactive Java service.
output_examples.output: A design recommendation using async ingestion, controlled concurrency, partial failure handling, and schema matching.

best_practices: Batch log records and reuse the ingestion client across requests.
Match log fields to the Data Collection Rule transformation schema.
Mask secrets before upload and handle partial failures explicitly.
anti_patterns: Uploading one log record per request during normal traffic.
Sending unfiltered request bodies, tokens, or customer data as log fields.
Ignoring 403 and 404 responses instead of checking permissions and identifiers.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure Monitor resources?
faq.answer: No. It assumes the Data Collection Endpoint, Data Collection Rule, workspace, and target table already exist.

faq.question: Which Java package does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK package com.azure:azure-monitor-ingestion.

faq.question: Can it help with asynchronous ingestion?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for LogsIngestionAsyncClient and Reactor-based upload flows.

faq.question: Does it explain partial upload failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to use upload error handling to inspect failed records and response exceptions.

faq.question: Is this only for custom tables?
faq.answer: No. The prerequisite notes mention custom tables and supported built-in tables such as Syslog and WindowsEvents.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare Azure authentication, a Data Collection Endpoint, a Data Collection Rule ID, a stream name, and matching log fields.
